title: ProgramClean: Zero-Shot Data Cleaning via LLM-Based Constraint Program Synthesis
description: We propose ProgramClean, a data cleaning framework that treats LLMs as compilers rather than detectors. Instead of using LLMs for per-cell inference (expensive) or learning constraints from corpora (requires training data), ProgramClean compiles column semantics into executable Python constraint programs through zero-shot LLM synthesis. This achieves semantic-aware cleaning at statistical-method speeds without requiring labeled examples, training corpora, RAG, few-shot examples, or per-cell LLM calls.
motivation: Current data cleaning methods face a fundamental trade-off: statistical methods (Raha, Baran, Auto-Test) are fast but semantically blind, while LLM methods (Cocoon, IterClean) are semantically aware but computationally expensive due to per-cell or per-batch inference. Auto-Test requires large pre-existing datasets; IterClean requires labeled tuples; Akella et al. uses RAG and iteratively-generated few-shot examples; SEED addresses general data curation but does not specifically target constraint-based cleaning. We need an approach that combines semantic awareness with computational efficiency without requiring any training data, labeled data, or external knowledge sources.
proposed_approach: ProgramClean uses a three-phase LLM-as-compiler approach: (1) Semantic Profiling - LLM analyzes column names, samples, and context to generate structured semantic profiles; (2) Constraint Program Synthesis - LLM compiles semantic profiles into unified executable Python programs containing coupled validation and repair functions (not individual validators per rule); (3) Scalable Execution - compiled programs run in sandboxed environments as native code without LLM dependency. This shifts LLM inference from O(cells) or O(batches) to O(columns), achieving statistical efficiency with LLM-level semantic understanding.
key_innovations: LLM-as-Compiler for Data Cleaning: Application of the LLM-as-compiler paradigm specifically for constraint-based data cleaning, distinct from SEED's general data curation focus
Unified Program Synthesis: Generates complete constraint programs with coupled detection and repair logic, not individual validators per rule
Truly Zero-Shot: Requires no labeled data, no training corpus, no RAG, and no few-shot examples—synthesizes programs purely from column metadata and samples
Security-Aware Execution: Systematic sandboxing strategy for executing LLM-generated constraint code with defense-in-depth controls
Failure Mode Analysis: Systematic characterization of semantic bug risks (syntactically valid but semantically incorrect programs) with mitigation strategies

related_work.title: Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables
related_work.authors: Yeye He, Raymond Chi-Wing Wong, et al.
related_work.distinction: Auto-Test learns constraints statistically from table corpora using large-scale statistical tests. ProgramClean synthesizes constraint programs using LLMs as compilers. Auto-Test requires large training corpora; ProgramClean works zero-shot without any corpus. Auto-Test does not use LLMs; ProgramClean leverages LLM semantic reasoning for program synthesis.

related_work.title: Data Cleaning Using Large Language Models
related_work.authors: Shuo Zhang, Zezhou Huang, Jia Guo, Dong Deng, Eugene Wu
related_work.distinction: Cocoon uses LLMs as detectors through iterative prompting for error detection on data batches. ProgramClean uses LLMs as compilers that generate constraint programs once per column. Cocoon requires per-batch LLM inference (expensive); ProgramClean shifts cost to compile-time (one-time per column).

related_work.title: IterClean: An Iterative Data Cleaning Framework with Large Language Models
related_work.authors: Wei Ni, Kaihang Zhang, Xiaoye Miao, Xiangyu Zhao, Yangyang Wu, Jianwei Yin
related_work.distinction: IterClean uses an iterative detector-verifier-repairer architecture with LLMs, requiring labeled tuples for few-shot prompting and performing per-batch LLM inference. ProgramClean requires no labeled data and performs LLM inference only once per column during program synthesis.

related_work.title: Quality Assessment of Tabular Data using Large Language Models and Code Generation
related_work.authors: Ashlesha Akella, Akshar Kaul, Krishnasuri Narayanam, Sameep Mehta
related_work.distinction: Akella et al. generate individual validators per rule using RAG and iteratively-generated few-shot examples. ProgramClean generates unified constraint programs per column (not per-rule), works truly zero-shot without RAG or examples, and synthesizes coupled detection-repair programs. Akella et al. generate detection-only validators; ProgramClean generates both detection and repair functions.

related_work.title: Domain-Specific Data Curation With Large Language Models
related_work.authors: Wenchao Cui, Lei Cao, Sam Madden, Tim Kraska, Zeyuan Shang, Ju Fan, Nan Tang, Zihui Gu, Chunwei Liu, Michael Cafarella
related_work.distinction: SEED presents an LLM-as-compiler approach for general data curation (entity resolution, annotation, imputation, discovery) generating hybrid pipelines with multiple modules. ProgramClean specifically targets constraint-based data cleaning with unified constraint program synthesis and coupled detection-repair logic. SEED requires natural language task descriptions; ProgramClean works zero-shot from column metadata.

related_work.title: Raha: A Configuration-Free Error Detection System / Baran: Effective Error Correction
related_work.authors: Mohammad Mahdavi, Ziawasch Abedjan, et al.
related_work.distinction: Raha and Baran use ensemble learning and transfer learning for configuration-free error detection/correction. These methods are fast but semantically blind—they detect statistical anomalies without semantic understanding. ProgramClean adds semantic awareness through LLM-synthesized constraint programs.

related_work.title: ZS4C: Zero-Shot Synthesis of Compilable Code for Incomplete Code Snippets Using LLMs
related_work.authors: Azmain Kabir, Shaowei Wang, Yuan Tian, Tse-Hsun Chen, Muhammad Asaduzzaman, Wenbin Zhang
related_work.distinction: ZS4C addresses zero-shot code synthesis for Stack Overflow snippets using LLM-validator collaboration. While demonstrating zero-shot synthesis, ZS4C addresses a different problem (code completion) and does not apply to data cleaning or constraint generation. ProgramClean applies zero-shot synthesis specifically to data quality constraints with unified detection-repair programs.

hypothesis: LLMs can effectively compile column semantics and value patterns into executable Python constraint programs that detect and repair errors with accuracy comparable to iterative LLM methods (Cocoon, IterClean) but with computational cost comparable to statistical methods (Raha/Baran).

confirmed_if: ProgramClean achieves F1 score within 10% of Cocoon on standard benchmarks (Hospital, Flights, Beers, Movies, Rayyan)
ProgramClean runs 100x faster than per-cell or per-batch LLM methods (measured by wall-clock time)
ProgramClean successfully handles semantic types not present in Auto-Test's training corpus (demonstrating zero-shot capability)
Synthesized programs execute without errors on >90% of test columns
Ablation study shows ProgramClean matches or exceeds Direct Validation accuracy (validating compilation value)
refuted_if: Synthesized constraint programs achieve <70% precision on standard benchmarks
LLM-synthesized programs have execution errors in >10% of cases
Zero-shot program synthesis fails to capture domain semantics (repair accuracy <50%)
Semantic bugs (correct syntax, wrong logic) occur in >20% of programs and escape detection

datasets: Hospital (Rekatsinas et al., 2017): 1,000 rows, typos and FD violations
Flights (Rekatsinas et al., 2017): Temporal data, FD violations
Beers (Mahdavi et al., 2019): Product data, semantic errors
Rayyan (Ouzzani et al., 2016): Systematic review data
Novel Domain Test: Cryptocurrency addresses, JWT tokens, modern TLDs
baselines: SEED: LLM-as-compiler for data curation (adapted for cleaning)
Raha + Baran: Statistical detection and correction
Akella et al. approach: Statistical inlier detection + LLM rule generation with RAG
ablations: Direct Validation: LLM validates each cell directly via prompting (no program synthesis)
Naive Code Gen: LLM generates code without semantic profiling step
metrics: Precision, Recall, F1 for error detection
Estimated cost for cloud LLM usage

failure_modes.semantic_bugs: LLM generates syntactically valid code with incorrect semantic constraints (e.g., wrong regex). Mitigated via self-validation on sample data, cross-validation with statistics, and synthetic violation testing.
failure_modes.overly_restrictive: Validators may reject valid but unusual values. Mitigated via statistical outlier detection alongside constraints and configurable strictness levels.
failure_modes.incorrect_repairs: Repairs may produce semantically invalid results. Mitigated via post-repair validation and edit distance limits.
failure_modes.execution_errors: Generated code may raise runtime exceptions. Mitigated via sandboxed execution, static analysis, and fallback to statistical methods.

security_considerations.risk: Executing LLM-generated Python code introduces risks: arbitrary code execution, file system access, data exfiltration
sandboxing_strategy: Process Isolation: Separate subprocesses with restricted privileges
Resource Limits: CPU time (30s), memory (512MB), recursion depth limits
Module Whitelist: Standard library + pandas/numpy only; network/file/OS modules blocked
Restricted Built-ins: eval(), exec(), compile(), __import__(), file I/O removed
Input Data Isolation: Operations on copies only, never in-place modification
validation_before_execution: Static Analysis: AST parsing for prohibited operations
Pattern Matching: Regex filtering for dangerous keywords
Fallback Strategy: Fall back to statistical methods if sandboxing fails
security_considerations.trade_off: Restrictions may prevent some legitimate complex constraints but ensure safe execution

computational_requirements.gpu: None required - LLM inference done once per column using smaller models
computational_requirements.llm_backend: Open-source models (Llama 3.1-8B, CodeLlama-7B) via vLLM on CPU
computational_requirements.time_budget: ~8 hours total for all experiments, scoped with smaller models and limited datasets

time_budget_justification: Given 8-hour CPU-only constraint: use smaller models (Llama 3.1-8B, CodeLlama-7B) for faster CPU inference; limit to 5 standard benchmarks (~50 columns); novel domain tests limited to 10 types; ablations on subset only. Estimated 6-7 hours for full suite.
expected_contributions: Application of LLM-as-compiler paradigm specifically for constraint-based data cleaning
True zero-shot unified constraint program synthesis (no labeled data, corpus, RAG, or few-shot)
Security-aware execution with systematic sandboxing strategy
Systematic failure mode analysis for LLM-synthesized constraints
Empirical evaluation including ablation on compilation value
limitations: Pre-trained knowledge dependence: Relies on LLM training data coverage
Sandboxing overhead: Security constraints may limit legitimate constraint complexity
Single-column focus: Multi-column dependencies require extension
CPU inference limits: Large-scale evaluation with SOTA models impractical without GPU
